title: Two Sides, One Story
description: Understanding starts with seeing both perspectives. Try to find their way forward (or not...) together.

person_b.name: B
person_b.tagline: Wants to maintain this connection [idk, nothing is impossible, never give up -> too stubborn though]

phases.name: Before The Conversation
phases.title: Where They Stand (Coming into the Conversation)
phases.description: Where each person stands before, and coming into the conversation.

Current Opinion.A: B wants his way too often. He is too pushy and keeps overstepping boundaries. I feel like this connection does not bring my joy and is draining me. I don't want to maintain this connection and I see no point in continuing.
Current Opinion.B: I want to mend this relationship because I care about this connection. I have reflected on my actions and know I can change in the way needed because I value our relationship. I am willing to listen and make changes if necessary as well, but I hope that Germaine can also see the value in what we have together. I understand that Germaine has concerns, but I also feel that there is potential for growth and improvement in our relationship.

What They Want.A: The relationship to end after this.
What They Want.B: The issues can be resolved and have the chance to work hard towards a future that allows us to maintain our connection. (So we can have casual light hearted talks and go together for sifa, theatre, photography, etc.)

What They Expect.A: A short conversation to clear up anything left unsaid.
What They Expect.B: A long and deep conversation where we can both express our feelings and concerns/problems so it can be resolved.

How They See the Future of this Relation.B: Meaningful (got value and potential to grow and be better).

Friends?.A: 不要，没理由，不想再见了，浪费时间。
Friends?.B: 要，把问题解决了，进步后，我们还可以当好朋友。比之前还要好，毕竟更了解彼此了，如果说了还不改，放弃（断绝）还可以。因为我不想失去这个朋友了。

phases.name: Present
phases.title: What They're Saying
phases.description: What they're actually trying to say to each other — the what, why, and how.

Trying to Say.A: Say what you want to say quickly. I dislike this connection. This relationship is draining and doesn't bring me joy. I have better things to do and want this to end.
Trying to Say.B: I want to hear the issues you have (ask questions to understand) and work on myself and to improve the relationship. I want to fix this connection because I care about it and see potential for growth.

Problem Surfaced.A: You are too pushy and overstep boundaries. I feel drained and unhappy in this relationship.
Problem Surfaced.B: I overstepped her boundaries, especially so with the Ipad incident. I also feel annoying to text so much when she doesnt want to reply and ghosts, like i should shut up alr.

Reaction to Problem/Opinion of the Other.A: He is not listening, I already said that I don't want to maintain this connection and he keeps trying to fix it. He is being pushy and just wants it his way.
Reaction to Problem/Opinion of the Other.B: I hear her, she thinks I am being too pushy and I overstepped boundaries. She feels drained talking to me and I don't bring her joy. She finds the relationship meaningless and does not want to maintain the connection. I understand the concerns she raised and I know these issues can be resolved and the relationship can be improved if I work on myself and we both put in the effort to make it work. However, I also understand that she has better things to do and does not want to waste time on this relationship.

Personal Thoughts.A: Bro annoying af, he just wants it his way and doesn't listen to me. I don't want to maintain this connection and I don't see any point in it. I have better things to do and I want this to end.
Personal Thoughts.B: I am being pushy in trying to fix and maintain the connection when she does not want to engage, and not giving her space when she wants it. I just don't want to give up and I believe it can work out. But what value is there in it for her? Why should she want to maintain this connection? If it meant sacrificing time on things she can find joy in, to take this risk in this relationship, why would she want to do that? Even if it can improve, why would she wait around for it or give it a chance when she can just do something else that brings her joy?

Why It Matters.A: I have hobbies I find more joy in and would also prefer spending time with other people who I find more enjoyable to be around. Why risk maintaining this connection and wait for the change or help out here, it does not bring me any joy or benefit
Why It Matters.B: If I give her space, I worry that it will be the permanent end of our relationship and that she will not want to maintain it at all. I am also worried that if I try to fix things, it will come across as me being pushy and not respecting her feelings and boundaries, which is not my intention at all. Honestly though? If she does not want to maintain this connection, there is no way to get through to her if she is not willing to give it a chance since she will not loosen up and everything will be 'sian' to her. Like fr, if you dont find the person fun and keep thinking about how you have better things to do, then no matter how fun the activity or any conversation is, you will never find it fun or anything, since you already have that predisposition to not find it fun. So if she is not willing to give it a chance and loosen up, its just gg here. Best analogy I can think of is, you know that guy you say he looks weird based on appearance and posture? because he is socially awkward? but even if he is a fun guy or cool guy, like he loves pokemon go, you will never find him fun or cool because you already have that predisposition to not find him fun or cool. So if you are not willing to give it a chance and loosen up, its just gg here.

How to Resolve.A: Put a full stop to this connection and end it after this conversation. I don't want to maintain this connection and I don't see any point in it. I have better things to do and I want this to end.
How to Resolve.B: I am open to trying a trial period or test run to see if we can improve the relationship and make it work. I think it could be a good way to give this a real shot and see if it brings us joy and benefits without fully giving up or accepting it right away. It would also give us both a chance to see if we can resolve the issues and make the relationship better before making a final decision on whether to maintain it or not.

points.Feeling: Alive. Validated. The world opened up and proved the leap was worth it.
points.Gained: New perspective, new network, renewed sense of possibility. The relationship feels dynamic again.
points.Growing Edge: If it doesn't work, the pressure to justify the sacrifice. The 'I told you so' that lingers unspoken.

points.Feeling: Uprooted but trying. Grief for what was left behind, mixed with cautious curiosity.
points.Gained: Proof of flexibility. Deeper understanding of what 'home' actually means to them.
points.Growing Edge: Resentment if things go badly. A lingering feeling of not being heard.

points.Feeling: Contained. Grateful for stability but haunted by 'what if.' The itch goes underground.
points.Gained: Security. Time to figure out if the urge to leave was about geography or something internal.
points.Growing Edge: The dream becomes a sore spot. Every bad day becomes 'evidence' they should have left.

points.Feeling: Relieved. Like the foundation held. Space to build upward without losing everything.
points.Gained: Deeper roots, stronger community, the satisfaction of choosing depth over novelty.
points.Growing Edge: Guilt about 'winning.' Anxiety the other person resents the choice.

points.Feeling: Heard. The door is open, not slammed shut. The possibility is real — that's enough for now.
points.Gained: A concrete plan instead of a daydream. The feeling of moving forward together.
points.Growing Edge: If the trial succeeds, the permanence question returns. If it fails — was it a real try?

points.Feeling: Safe. A trial has an exit ramp — it's not a one-way door. This feels fair.
points.Gained: Time. Data. A chance to experience the new without burning the old.
points.Growing Edge: Limbo. Two half-lives instead of one whole one. The exhaustion of holding two realities.
